title: Sweets And Sour Marge

jokes.text: D'oh!
jokes.setup: Homer's opening catchphrase sets the tone
jokes.punchline: The iconic frustrated exclamation
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer catchphrase, perfectly economical and character-specific but not particularly original anymore

jokes.text: A library selling books? If I don't want them for free, why would I want to pay for them?
jokes.punchline: Homer's backwards logic about free vs. paid books
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer logic - the setup and delivery work perfectly to establish his character's anti-intellectual stance

jokes.text: The full Leonard Nimoy cycle, I Am Not Spock, then I Am Spock, and finally, I Am Also Scotty
jokes.setup: Discussing Leonard Nimoy's real career arc with his Spock identity
jokes.punchline: The absurd final book title suggesting he claims to be Scotty too
jokes.explanation: Clever meta-joke about actor identity crisis, perfectly suited to Comic Book Guy's pedantic character

jokes.text: That's what we look like inside? It's disgusting! Oh. That lady swallowed a baby!
jokes.punchline: Misunderstanding pregnancy as baby-swallowing
jokes.explanation: Child-like misunderstanding played well for both disgust and misdirection

jokes.text: Helen Fielding's giving them pigs Bridget Jones' diarrhea
jokes.setup: Discussion about books being fed to pigs
jokes.punchline: Pun on 'Bridget Jones' Diary' becoming diarrhea
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay but feels slightly forced for Lisa's character

jokes.text: She said tavern! I'm going to Moe's! I never agreed to that rule!
jokes.setup: Innocent mention of taverns in historical context
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately using it as excuse to drink, with Marge's frustrated response
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer behavior - any excuse to drink, with Marge's exasperated but resigned response

jokes.text: Most clothespins swallowed, inserted... Here we go. Clipped to face and neck. 116.
jokes.punchline: The disturbing progression from face clipping to more invasive categories
jokes.explanation: Escalating absurdity of record categories with dark implications

jokes.text: No. The best book you've ever seen is Tom Clancy's Op-Center. That thing knows me better than I know myself.
jokes.punchline: Moe finding deep personal meaning in a Tom Clancy novel
jokes.explanation: Funny contrast between Moe's tough exterior and finding deep meaning in airport thriller

jokes.text: Francis of Assisi. Francis of Assisi.
jokes.setup: Reading about world's most overrated saint
jokes.punchline: Moe's matter-of-fact agreement with the controversial opinion
jokes.explanation: Mild religious humor with Moe's characteristic bluntness

jokes.text: Do you think I could run a mile in three-and-a-half minutes? Only on Mars. The Simpsons are going to Mars! So pack your... Or maybe I'll think of something else.
jokes.setup: Homer asks about impossible running time
jokes.punchline: Lisa's physics joke leads to Homer's wild enthusiasm then immediate abandonment
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Homer's short attention span and wild enthusiasm

jokes.text: Only on Mars. / The Simpsons are going to Mars! So pack your... / Or maybe I'll think of something else.
jokes.setup: Lisa's sarcastic comment about Homer running fast
jokes.punchline: Homer's instant pivot from Mars expedition excitement to deflation
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer enthusiasm immediately deflated by reality

jokes.text: Technically the cobra would get the record. He's the one playing.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to play banjo with cobra
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic logic applied to absurd situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant bureaucratic pedantry applied to completely absurd scenario

jokes.text: Yeah. They're called Congress!
jokes.setup: Official mentions people with no discernible talent
jokes.punchline: Homer's political jab at Congress
jokes.explanation: Standard political humor, well-delivered but not particularly original

jokes.text: Like that town that made the world's largest omelet. Denver? No, Spanish.
jokes.punchline: Confusion between Denver and Spanish omelet type
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay that works but isn't particularly memorable

jokes.text: Finally this town will have a real claim to fame. Aye. We can stop all the lies.
jokes.punchline: Willie's acknowledgment they've been lying about their accomplishments
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about Springfield's lack of real accomplishments

jokes.text: I haven't seen this much flesh since Elke Sommer's hot tub party. Seriously, Elke's great. Always there for sick kids. Doing great things.
jokes.setup: Mayor making seemingly inappropriate comment about flesh
jokes.punchline: Unexpected wholesome praise for the celebrity
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up for sleazy comment but delivers genuine respect

jokes.text: All right, I'll make base camp here and try for the summit tomorrow.
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy treating it like mountain climbing expedition
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy - taking the mundane and making it epic/dramatic

jokes.text: Dude, you're touching my hand! Ewe!
jokes.setup: Pyramid participants holding position for record
jokes.punchline: Otto's discomfort with human contact causing collapse
jokes.explanation: Otto's character moment but fairly predictable physical comedy setup

jokes.text: Goodbye, cruel world. Hello, ironic twist!
jokes.punchline: Comic Book Guy's self-aware commentary on the situation
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke - Comic Book Guy recognizing story structure in real time, perfect character integration

jokes.text: I am so far from my car. Dude, you're lying on top of me! Dude?
jokes.punchline: Otto's practical concern about his car distance and awkward physical position
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy aftermath, Otto's character priorities showing through

jokes.text: Springfield is the world's fattest town!
jokes.punchline: Discovering they accidentally set a different, embarrassing record
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection and ironic twist - they got a record but not the one they wanted

jokes.text: Woo-hoo! In your face, Milwaukee!
jokes.punchline: Homer celebrates this embarrassing distinction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer response - proud of something he should be ashamed of, with random Milwaukee rivalry

jokes.text: This town will no longer be known as 'America's Sorrow.' Today I declare Springfield 'Fat City, USA!'
jokes.setup: Mayor trying to put positive spin on fattest town designation
jokes.punchline: Revealing Springfield's previous embarrassing nickname and creating new embarrassing one
jokes.explanation: Good political spin humor revealing Springfield's perpetual problems

jokes.text: Now that everyone's so open about being fat, I can finally stop sucking in my gut.
jokes.punchline: Carl's relief at not having to hide his weight anymore
jokes.explanation: Relatable observational humor about body image and social acceptance

jokes.text: Mom, we're out of Frosting Gobs. Here, have one of my fudge-stuffed toaster pies.
jokes.punchline: Revealing absurdly sugary food names and casual attitude toward them
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the sugar theme with absurd product names

jokes.text: Yeah. You can't improve on nature.
jokes.setup: Marge suggests Homer eat healthy grapefruit
jokes.punchline: Homer's agreement while presumably doctoring the fruit with sugar
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer irony - stating healthy principle while violating it

jokes.text: Sugar-free donuts! No, that is sugar with free donuts.
jokes.setup: Homer excited about apparent health option
jokes.punchline: Apu's correction revealing it's the opposite - pure sugar with complimentary donuts
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay and misdirection showing the absurd extent of sugar addiction

jokes.text: Is my carton of Pixy Stix in? No, it hasn't come in yet. Damn it! When they come in, you call me at this number.
jokes.punchline: Barney treating candy like a drug dealer transaction
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Barney's addictive personality transferred to sugar

jokes.text: everything in this store, from the honey-glazed cauliflower to the Choco-Blasted Baby Aspirin
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd items with sugar added, culminating in medication
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd sugar-added products, especially baby aspirin

jokes.text: Hey, Marge. I'm not up on the current slang, but do the kids still say, 'Get the hell out of my office'?
jokes.punchline: Treating hostile dismissal as trendy slang
jokes.explanation: Excellent deadpan delivery making rudeness seem like generational confusion

jokes.text: While we're at it, why don't I just change my name back to Hitler?
jokes.setup: Marge suggesting health warnings will boost sales
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic escalation to Hitler comparison
jokes.explanation: Dark humor showing corporate callousness, but Hitler comparison is somewhat lazy

jokes.text: But we're on hiatus. Everyone's everywhere. Note to Marge. Get out!
jokes.setup: Executive tells Marge to take it up with her sewing circle
jokes.punchline: Marge's literal response about scheduling and executive's dictation habit
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Marge's literal-mindedness against corporate dismissiveness

jokes.text: If you looked up meanie-beanie-fo-feanie in the dictionary, you'd see his picture.
jokes.punchline: Her childlike insult construction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge - expressing anger in the most wholesome way possible

jokes.text: Were there Oompa Loompas? There was one in a cage, but he wasn't moving.
jokes.setup: Bart asking about sugar factory with Willy Wonka reference
jokes.punchline: Dark implication about captive/dead Oompa Loompa
jokes.explanation: Dark twist on beloved children's movie reference with efficient delivery

jokes.text: Oh, yeah! Like Erin Brockovich. The prostitute with the heart of gold.
jokes.punchline: Homer's confused movie reference mixing up character types
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer confusion mixing up different movie archetypes

jokes.text: Today's law schools are churning out 2.1 lawyers for every person in America
jokes.punchline: Lisa's statistical exaggeration about lawyer oversupply
jokes.explanation: Lisa's smart-kid observation with slight exaggeration for effect

jokes.text: Pick me! Pick me! Me so litigious! Pick me! I just passed the bar, see?
jokes.punchline: Broken English parody and literal bar reference
jokes.explanation: Crowd scene humor with mild wordplay, functional but not exceptional

jokes.text: Oh, I've made too many enemies selling suckless vacuum cleaners and Rick James Bibles.
jokes.punchline: Revealing his history of selling absurd products
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gil moment - combining his loser status with absurdist product names

jokes.text: Sugar's made my Ralphie hyperactive. I'm happy and angry!
jokes.punchline: Ralph's contradictory emotional state
jokes.explanation: Good Ralph moment capturing child-like confusion about complex emotions

jokes.text: Sugar's made my Ralphie hyperactive. / I'm happy and angry!
jokes.punchline: Ralph's contradictory emotional state
jokes.explanation: Classic Ralph nonsensical statement - perfectly captures child's emotional confusion

jokes.text: Figure them sugar folk owe me for what they done to my cousin, Dia-Betty.
jokes.punchline: Name that's clearly diabetes-related
jokes.explanation: Good Cletus moment with name pun that fits his dialect and situation

jokes.text: I'm trying to slim down so I can fit into Momma's coffin. That's my reward.
jokes.punchline: Morbid goal of fitting into mother's coffin as incentive
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor perfectly suited to Comic Book Guy's morbid character

jokes.text: Disco Stu got hooked on the white stuff back in the 70s.
jokes.setup: Disco Stu explaining his sugar problems
jokes.punchline: Making sugar addiction sound like cocaine addiction
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection playing on drug terminology while staying true to character

jokes.text: If it isn't the woman who's too busy saving the world to save her own marriage.
jokes.setup: Marge returns home to find Homer upset
jokes.punchline: Homer's passive-aggressive guilt trip
jokes.explanation: Good marital dynamic humor showing Homer's selfishness disguised as hurt

jokes.text: Why didn't you just turn off the oven? I was hoping we could do that together!
jokes.setup: Marge asking logical question about burned dinner
jokes.punchline: Homer's ridiculous expectation of shared mundane activity
jokes.explanation: Excellent absurdist humor - making oven operation into romantic activity

jokes.text: Out of curiosity, was it the hoyvin or the mayvin or was it the whole gahoyving thing that I do?
jokes.setup: Someone identifying him as Professor Frink
jokes.punchline: His self-aware analysis of his own speech patterns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-humor - character analyzing his own catchphrase with more catchphrases

jokes.text: Candy was just a sugar delivery system. We thought we were God.
jokes.punchline: Dramatic admission of playing God through candy
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about corporate callousness with dramatic flair

jokes.text: I'll kill you while you sleep. Objection! I'll allow it.
jokes.punchline: Judge allowing inadmissible death threat as evidence
jokes.explanation: Good legal parody showing incompetent courtroom proceedings

jokes.text: but I had to quit when my fangs succumbed to gingivitis. Now all my victims have to be mashed up.
jokes.punchline: Dental problems affecting vampire feeding with disturbing implications
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark humor combining dental health with vampire mythology

jokes.text: Sir, this is a house of justice, not a sugar shack.
jokes.punchline: Judge's pun on musical reference while maintaining dignity
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay on 'Love Shack' while maintaining judicial authority

jokes.text: Thank you, Erin Choco-snitch! That was a group effort. Well, good work, Blue-hair-in-Brocko-witch. Okay, that was mine.
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate puns on Erin Brockovich with competitive element
jokes.explanation: Decent wordplay sequence showing media's tendency to create catchy negative nicknames

jokes.text: Even this promotional Johnny Depp from the movie Chocolat? We melted for him. Now he's gonna do likewise.
jokes.setup: Burning sugar products including celebrity tie-ins
jokes.punchline: Pun on romantic attraction meaning versus literal melting
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on melting/attraction with current movie reference

jokes.text: It's not even singed. Even the fire doesn't want them.
jokes.punchline: Anthropomorphizing fire's rejection of the candy
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor about universally disliked candy with personification

jokes.text: They're not riding the white horse anymore.
jokes.setup: Observing withdrawal symptoms from sugar ban
jokes.punchline: Using drug addiction terminology for sugar withdrawal
jokes.explanation: Willie's poetic way of describing sugar withdrawal using drug metaphors

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, you're licking blood and VapoRub. Part of me knew that.
jokes.punchline: His awareness but continued action despite disgusting mixture
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor showing Homer's desperation and self-awareness of his poor choices

jokes.text: I'm a monster! Don't look at me!
jokes.setup: Count Fudgula joining sugar smuggling operation
jokes.punchline: Vampire mascot having existential crisis about addiction
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing mascot's self-loathing about his role in addiction

jokes.text: Oh, you mean Tennessee? No, the island of San Glucose.
jokes.setup: Discussion of smuggling from 'south of the border'
jokes.punchline: Homer's geographical confusion and fake sugar island name
jokes.explanation: Good Homer geography confusion with clever sugar-themed island name

jokes.text: Dessert's on! I steamed some limes!
jokes.setup: Family dealing with sugar ban at dinner
jokes.punchline: Marge's pathetic attempt at sugar-free dessert
jokes.explanation: Perfect Marge moment - trying to be positive about terrible sugar-free alternatives

jokes.text: I think he's grossed out by some seaweed. Ew! I touched it! Over.
jokes.punchline: His disgust at seaweed while on dangerous mission
jokes.explanation: Homer's misplaced priorities during serious mission showing his character

jokes.text: Has everyone keistered their personal supply? 'Cause I sure haven't.
jokes.punchline: Homer's innocent admission about not hiding contraband properly
jokes.explanation: Adult humor with Homer's innocent delivery of crude concept

jokes.text: No! I'm having one. Remember what I told you about running away from your troubles? Yeah. Let's do it!
jokes.setup: Moe shouting rowing commands during chase
jokes.punchline: Revealing he's having a stroke, leading to immediate abandonment of heroics
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and character consistency - Moe's health crisis leading to cowardice

jokes.text: This sugar shark is delicious. Ow! Hey, he bit me back!
jokes.setup: Sugar in water creating sugar sharks
jokes.punchline: Captain eating one that fights back
jokes.explanation: Visual gag with sea captain's expected reaction to marine life

jokes.text: Indeed, Marge. And now that I think of it, I wildly exceeded my authority, and I declare the sugar ban over!
jokes.punchline: Judge's casual admission of judicial overreach and immediate reversal
jokes.explanation: Good legal satire about arbitrary judicial decisions and power abuse

jokes.text: No, Marge. I love when you do that. You're a regular Caring-a-lot-ovich.
jokes.punchline: Homer's supportive but butchered attempt at Erin Brockovich reference
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Homer's characteristic mangling of references while being supportive

jokes.text: Hey, I found some pearls. No, wait. They're just my teeth. Well, I can still make a necklace out of them.
jokes.setup: Sea Captain in sugar water looking for valuables
jokes.punchline: Mistaking his own teeth for pearls but staying optimistic
jokes.explanation: Dark humor with Sea Captain's optimistic perspective on dental loss

tmdb_overview: Springfield is officially declared the World's Fattest Town after an attempt to break a world record lands everyone on top of a truck scale. Out of embarrassment and disgust, Marge goes on a crusade against the local sugar corporation. However, when sugar is banned, Homer, Bart, Mr. Burns and Apu start bootlegging sugar.
